subject: Statistics
title: Causal Impact Analysis
description: A micro-lesson that makes Causal Impact Analysis usable.

hook.text: A company launches an ad campaign and sales rise 15%. Was it the ad, or would sales have risen anyway?

buildup.text: Google's CausalImpact builds a forecast of what would have happened without the intervention — the counterfactual.

discovery.text: The gap between the actual data and the counterfactual forecast estimates the causal effect of the intervention.

twist.text: The method needs control time series — similar metrics unaffected by the intervention — to build the counterfactual.

climax.text: Causal impact analysis turns time series data into causal evidence without randomized experiments.

punchline.text: What would have happened without the change? The counterfactual answers it.

quiz.question: How does causal impact analysis estimate the effect of an intervention?
options: It compares actual data to a counterfactual forecast of what would have happened without the intervention
It only looks at data before the intervention
